name: Jihad denotes doing one's utmost to achieve something. It is not the equivalent of war
introduction:
for which the Arabic word is gital. Jihad has a wider connotation and embraces every kind
of striving in God's cause. A mujahid is a person who is single-mindedly devoted to his
cause, who uses his mental capacity to reflect how best he can achieve it, propagates it by
word of mouth and by the pen, uses his physical energy in striving to serve it, spends all the
resources at his disposal to promote it, employs all the force he commands in confronting
any power which might stand in its way, and, whenever necessary, does not shirk risking
his very life for it. All this is Jihad. ' Jihad in the way of God' is that strife in which man
engages exclusively to win God's good pleasure, to establish the supremacy of His religion
and to make His word prevail.
